SpTU 2, 015 [Šurpu 8][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
(1 column missing)
Column ii
(start of column missing)
o ii 1'o 1'

[...] x ⸢ḪUL-MEŠ ša⸣ [...]

[...] the evil things of [...]

o ii 2'2'

[... it-ta]-⸢nab⸣-šu-u₂ it-ta-nam-[ma-ru]

[...] happen repeatedly, are seen again and again,

o ii 3'3'

[... lu]-u₂ pa-as-sa-[nik-ka]

[...] may they be erased [for you].

Reverse
Column i
r i 1r i 1

⸢KI ma⸣-mit

A.ŠA₃ gišKIRI₆ E₂ SILA su-la-a ⸢ib⸣-[ra-tu₄ ...]

With the curse (related to)

field, garden, house, street, alley, cult niche, [...];

r i 22

⸢KI⸣ ma-mit

gišGI gišTIR qa-nu-u₂ ḫa-ṣa-bu šam-⸢mu⸣ [...]

With the curse (related to)

reed-bed, forest, the breaking off of reeds, [...] of plants [...];

r i 33

⸢KI⸣ ma-mit

gišAPIN gišTUKUL ḫar-bu ši-ri-iʾ mi-ṣir ⸢NIG₂⸣.[DU ...]

With the curse (related to)

seed plough, plough blade, furrow, border, boundary [...];

r i 44

⸢KI⸣ ma-mit

i-ki pal-gu ti-tur-ru me-te-⸢qu⸣ [...]

With the curse (related to)

dyke, ditch, bridge, passage, [...];

r i 55

⸢KI⸣ ma-mit

gišMA₂ <<ina>> ID₂ ka-a-ri ne₂-be₂-ri šu-x [...]

With the curse (related to)

boat, canal, quay, crossing, ... [...];

r i 66

⸢KI⸣ ma-mit

bi-li-ḫe-e kup-pi na-ḫal u ma-ḫa-[zi ...]

With the curse (related to)

Balihu, water-source, stream and spring [...];

r i 77

⸢KI⸣ ma-mit

du-u₂-tu₂ a-lu E₂ gišNIG₂.GIDRU še-be₂-ri tur-tu₄ ma-⸢mit⸣ [...]

With the curse (related to)

the secret place of city (and) house, the breaking of a stick, reversion of an oath, [...];

r i 88

⸢KI⸣ ma-mit

mi-ḫir GU₄ ṣe-na a-me-lu-ut-ti a-ma-⸢ru?⸣ [...]

With the curse (related to)

seeing the equivalent of oxen, flock and servants [...];

r i 99

⸢KI ma⸣-mit

ŠEŠ it-ba-ri ru-u₈-a tap-pu-u u₂-bar DUMU IRI na-⸢za⸣-[ru ...]

With the curse (related to)

cursing brother, friend, acquaintance, mate, foreigner, citizen [...];

r i 1010

[KI ma]-⸢mit⸣

ŠEŠ GALu₂ NIN GALti AD u AMA na-za-ri u na-[ka-ru ...]

[With] the curse (related to)

cursing elder brother, elder sister, father and mother, but denying it [...];

r i 1111

[KI ma-mit]

gišBANŠUR še-be₂-ri GU₂.ZI ḫa-pu-u₂? u niš DINGIR-MEŠ ⸢za⸣-[ka-ru ...]

[With the curse (related to)]

breaking an offering table, smashing a cup, swearing on the life of the gods [...];

r i 1212

[KI ma-mit]

gišGU.ZA šub-tu₂ gišNU₂ ma-a-a-al-tu₂ u [...]

[With the curse (related to)]

chair, seat, bed, litter and [...];

r i 1313

[KI ma-mit]

tug₂GU₂.E₃ na-kas₂ du-di-it-ti še-be₂-ri u ⸢di⸣-[da ...]

[With the curse (related to)]

cutting a nahlaptu-garment, breaking a dress-pin and [...] a dīdū-garment [...];

r i 1414

[KI ma-mit]

⸢TUG₂⸣.SIG₂ ba-ta-qu u GIR₂ ⸢AN⸣.[BAR ...]

[With the curse (related to)]

cutting off a hem and [...] an iron sword [...];

r i 1515

[KI ma-mit]

[ina] ⸢giš⸣BAN₂ TURti na-da-nu ina gišBAN₂ GAL⸢ti₃?⸣ [...]

[With the curse (related to)]

giving by the small sūtu-unit, [...] by the [...] sūtu-unit [...];

r i 1616

[KI ma-mit]

[ina 1] ⸢GIN₂⸣ ṣa-ḫar na-da-nu ina 1 GIN₂ GAL[i ...]

[With the curse (related to)]

giving [by 1] tiny shekel, [...] by 1 large shekel [...];

r i 1717

[KI ma-mit]

[...] ⸢ṣa?-ḫar na-da-nu⸣ ina ⸢1 MA⸣.[NA ...]

[With the curse (related to)]

giving [...] tiny [..., ...] by 1 [...] mina [...];
